chinese_news.title: 「维新宇航」完成数千万元天使+轮融资，7座3吨级eVTOL将于7月进行首飞测试｜36氪首发
chinese_news.summary:
文 | 阿至

36氪获悉，低空出行产品研制商「维新宇航」已完成数千万元天使+轮融资，本轮融资由策源资本领投、金舵投资跟投，资金将主要用于加速公司首款主力机型 Vector 5的研发迭代、首飞测试与适航取证进程。

维新宇航成立于2023年，是36氪长期关注的企业，其专注于大载客量、大载重eVTOL/eCTOL产品的研发及制造。加上6个月前连续完成的种子轮、天使轮，目前维新宇航已完成三轮融资，累计资金交割总额超亿元人民币。

现阶段，维新宇航的主力机型Vector 5为一款7座3吨级纯电复合翼eVTOL，该机型专注于应急救援和医疗急救等民生刚需场景，最大起飞重量为3180kg、最大载荷为680kg，最大航程300km、最高巡航速度为 250km/h，全尺寸样机已于去年6月正式下线。

chinese_news.title: 卡位黄金价格带，问界M6向下争夺年轻群体｜最前线
chinese_news.summary:
2026年的中国新能源SUV市场正经历着前所未有的内卷。

问界依托华为技术体系，推出了定位更亲民的全新车型 M6， 瞄准主流家用消费群体。放在问界品牌整体销量连续数月下滑、赛力斯盈利能力承压的背景下，M6的到来既是“救兵”，更是一场不容有失的“大考”。

卡位黄金价格带，向下争夺年轻群体

从产品命名逻辑看，M6位于M5与M7之间，车身尺寸为4960×1985×1736mm，轴距2950mm，属于标准的中大型五座SUV。官方定位为“新锐智慧SUV”，目标用户从问界以往的主力“30岁以上家庭用户和商务人群”向下延伸至25-35岁城市年轻群体。

25-30万元是目前中国新能源SUV市场竞争最激烈的价格带——特斯拉Model Y、理想L6、小鹏G6、比亚迪唐等车型均在此区间鏖战。作为参考，理想L6起售价24.98万元，小鹏G6起售价20.99万元，Model Y后驱版26.35万元。问界M6以25.98万元起售，锚定的正是理想L6的核心区间，试图以更低门槛的增程方案和更激进的全系标配策略争夺年轻家庭用户。

chinese_news.title: 小红书四年AI 路：FOMO、犹豫，到突然加速
chinese_news.summary:
作者 | 肖思佳

编辑 | 乔芊 杨轩

所有互联网大中厂都渴望在AI时代博得位置，在这场比赛中，小红书曾是克制的那个。

在一个搜索属性与社区属性并存，以真实经验分享为核心的产品中，活人感与AI、温情和算法，始终像天平的两端。

很长一段时间里，小红书既没有完全缺席技术探索，也没有像许多同行那样高调推进AI产品化。相反，这家公司始终在两股力量的拉锯和平衡中前行：一边持续投入模型能力，一边谨慎控制AI对社区生态的介入。

但2026年，随着Agent叙事的升温，小红书开始显露出某种急迫。

4月30日，小红书发送全员内部信，宣布成立AI一级部门Dots，“建立从模型研发、基础设施、工程到产品的完整技术体系，整合顶尖AI人才和资源。”Dots向小红书新任总裁柯南汇报。

据36氪了解，Dots部门由原人文智能实验室Hi Lab升级而来，下设模型研发、基础设施、工程、产品四个部门。目前小红书内部最重要的AI应用产品“点点”也被纳入该

chinese_news.title: 第一代全固态电池迈入规模化市场，「纯锂新能源」完成数千万元Pre-A+轮融资 | 36氪首发
chinese_news.summary:
文 | 张冰冰

编辑 | 阿至

36氪获悉，北京纯锂新能源科技有限公司（以下简称「纯锂新能源」）近日宣布完成数千万元Pre-A+轮融资，投资方为亦庄国投，云道资本担任独家财务顾问，资金将用于产品研发、市场拓展及产线落地。

「纯锂新能源」成立于2022年5月，聚焦全固态电池研发，通过自研复合电解质及超临界包覆工艺，破解全固态电池核心固固接触难题。2023年4月在北京亦庄建成小试线，量产10Ah全固态电芯；2023年5月在河南兰考县国电投新能源产业园启动建设500MWh规模量产线；2024年10月实现50Ah全固态电芯量产。

「纯锂新能源」于2025年推出第一代固态电池，全固态产品平均失重率0.023%，产品通过第三方国家标准实验室安全认证（CNAS）GB/T36276-2018 检测，目前正优先推进储能场景落地，并在低速动力等方向开展验证，为后续进入更广泛动力电池市场积累工程与商业化基础。

chinese_news.title: 36氪首发 | 清华系AI Infra厂商完成数亿元融资，以GPU为核心重构计算机系统架构
chinese_news.summary:
作者 | 乔钰杰

编辑 | 袁斯来

硬氪获悉，北京容芯致远科技有限公司（以下简称“容芯致远”）近日完成天使轮数亿元融资。本轮由北京绿色能源和低碳产业基金与赛富投资基金领投，顺禧基金、富华资本、万利达集团、长江创新投、水木清华校友基金、梅花创投等跟投。云岫资本此前参与了公司种子轮投资，本轮继续跟投，并担任长期独家财务顾问。

AI浪潮下，算力需求激增，传统以CPU为中心的架构瓶颈愈发凸显：CPU成为数据调度与交互的核心限制，GPU之间通信效率不足，内存无法实现统一地址空间共享，整体算力利用率较低。

容芯致远创始人石旭毕业于清华大学电子工程系，在芯片设计与AI领域从业多年，接受硬氪采访时，石旭表示，“在实际部署中，典型的AI服务器配置需要多个CPU协同调度少量GPU，且随着规模扩大，CPU数量也需同步增加，系统复杂度与成本显著上升，这正说明传统架构难以适配AI时代的计算需求。”

基于此，容芯致远提出了以GPU为核心的AI计算体系——AGC（A

chinese_news.title: Plaud获头部大厂投资，目前估值达20亿美元｜硬氪独家
chinese_news.summary:
作者｜黄楠 周鑫雨

编辑｜袁斯来 杨轩

硬氪独家获悉，AI卡片录音笔公司Plaud已于2025年年中拿下腾讯的融资，估值达10亿美元；目前，Plaud公司估值已涨至约20亿美元。同时，硬氪从相关人士处了解到，Plaud正在推进与腾讯会议之间的硬件合作。

有关上述信息，硬氪向Plaud及腾讯方面求证，双方均表示消息不实。

一名接近Plaud人士告诉硬氪，Plaud在2024年的总营收约5600万美金，利润率接近20%，“2025年营收差不多涨了3倍”。另有知情者透露，2025年Plaud全年收入达到了历年最高，大陆全线产品的出货量符合内部预期。

但Plaud出货量谈不上高，有一位行业人士告诉硬氪，其销量在国内市场或未超过十万台。显然，Plaud很难复刻过去几年在海外顺风顺水的爆涨。

Plaud的崛起，踩中了ChatGPT引爆的AI录音硬件风口。2023年，苹果手机海外市场无法

TOP3大新闻

“很久没有这种兴奋感了”，段永平再加仓泡泡玛特

5月7日，知名投资人段永平在社交平台发文称，把中国神华全部换成了泡泡玛特。值得注意的是，段永平此前已经多次表达了对泡泡玛特的看好，并直言“很久没有这种兴奋感了”，此次是首次明确已实际建仓泡泡玛特。此前通过卖出看跌期权的方式布局。段永平还表示：“我理解王宁（泡泡玛特创始人）不是因为我的投资，而是因为我曾经是个‘企业家’，我能看懂他有多厉害。他还那么年轻，他还能至少好好干25年以上吧。这个复利是吓人的。”目前，段永平还把社交媒体的头像换成了带有泡泡玛特产品的照片。（第一财经）

chinese_news.title: 在模型厂碾压之前，AI视频Agent产品是否只能挣波快钱？
chinese_news.summary:
文｜王毓婵 周鑫雨

编辑｜杨轩

“看流水（即营收），AI视频类这些项目的表现确实很不错，可以说是AI最赚钱的细分赛道之一。”投资行业人士对36氪说。 

中国 AI 视频生成赛道，正在经历大厂模型能力疯狂增长的巨大红利。来自字节跳动的Seedance和来自快手的可灵这两款“超级底座”正在进行一周一小版、两月一大版的高频迭代。阿里巴巴也在4月底，对视频生成模型HappyHorse 1.0开启灰测，720P视频生成刊例价为0.9元/秒。 

愿意为此花钱的内容创作者太多了，太急迫了。众多短剧、内容公司排队等待使用Seedance2.0已经成为2026年AI世界的一个奇观。由此，在AI视频模型外“套一层壳”，使其更简单易上手的AI视频Agent产品，也迎来了增长奇迹。

一位业内人士对智能涌现透露，头部公司一个月的算力消耗成本应该在百万元以上。 “一部短剧的算力消耗成本约为3万元，工具平台如果一个月能接100部这样的工程，那么消耗量就能达到300万。这没什么难度，只是时间问题。”</

prediction_markets_to_watch.market_title: Will Bitcoin remain above $75,000 on June 30, 2026?
prediction_markets_to_watch.why_now: BTC retreated below $80K today on $300M liquidations; Iran-US tensions and gas price inflation (May consumer sentiment at record low) create macro headwinds, but U.S. jobs beat (115K vs 55K expected) and stablecoin adoption growth (+100% YoY card spend) offer conflicting signals.
prediction_markets_to_watch.signal_source: CoinDesk (BTC below $80K, liquidations), CNBC (consumer sentiment low, jobs beat, Iran tensions), stablecoin velocity data

prediction_markets_to_watch.market_title: Will the Fed cut rates before September 30, 2026?
prediction_markets_to_watch.why_now: U.S. payrolls jumped more than expected (115K) but CNBC flagged 'red flags for the economy'; combined with record-low consumer sentiment from Iran-driven gas prices, markets now pricing probability of rate cuts amid conflicting labor/inflation signals.
prediction_markets_to_watch.signal_source: CNBC (jobs beat, red flags for economy, consumer sentiment record low), Fed policy expectations

prediction_markets_to_watch.market_title: Will Trump-EU trade deal be signed by July 4, 2026?
prediction_markets_to_watch.why_now: Trump just gave EU until July 4 deadline for trade ratification, threatening 'much higher' tariffs; this is a concrete, market-moving binary with only ~2 months to resolution and real tariff escalation risk.

prediction_markets_to_watch.market_title: Will Anthropic Mythos cause a material increase in cyber insurance claims by December 31, 2026?
prediction_markets_to_watch.why_now: Anthropic's Mythos cybersecurity tool has triggered 'hysteria' among banks and governments; Canvas (academic software) breach by ShinyHunters (12 countries, HN score 817) and $101M crypto wrench attacks in Europe show attack surface is live.
prediction_markets_to_watch.signal_source: CNBC (Mythos cybersecurity reckoning), BBC (Canvas breach, hantavirus cruise ship cyber tracking), Cointelegraph (Europe wrench attacks $101M), Hacker News (Canvas down, 817 score)

prediction_markets_to_watch.market_title: Will DRAM (Roundhill Memory ETF) stay above $2B AUM on August 31, 2026?
prediction_markets_to_watch.why_now: DRAM added $1B in a single day and has raised $5B since April 2 launch; this is the hottest ETF since Bitcoin mania, but momentum-driven inflows are vulnerable to reversal if memory chip capex cycles slow.

market_questions_to_create.question: Will Bitcoin exceed $95,000 by September 15, 2026?
market_questions_to_create.why_underpriced: Current retreat below $80K is driven by short-term liquidations and macro uncertainty (Iran, gas prices), but stablecoin adoption accelerating (+100% YoY), U.S. labor market surprisingly resilient, and institutional on-ramps (Coinbase crypto legislation momentum) create asymmetric upside if geopolitical tensions ease.
market_questions_to_create.trigger_signal: Stablecoin card spend growth, congressional crypto legislation traction, Iran ceasefire stabilization, ETH strength relative to BTC

market_questions_to_create.question: Will Ethereum close above $3,000 on October 31, 2026?
market_questions_to_create.why_underpriced: Cointelegraph reports 'Ethereum's rally exhausted at $2.4K' with four bearish signs, but this mirrors late 2024 sentiment before rallies; Ondo (ONDO) trending and institutional crypto adoption via stablecoins creates base for ETH upside.
market_questions_to_create.trigger_signal: Ondo (RWA tokenization), Starknet (STRK) scaling adoption, institutional stablecoin demand, Anthropic/AI infrastructure consolidation around Ethereum L2s

market_questions_to_create.question: Will the U.S. unemployment rate exceed 4.5% by November 30, 2026?
market_questions_to_create.resolution_source: U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics (monthly non-farm payrolls release)
market_questions_to_create.why_underpriced: Today's jobs beat (115K vs 55K expected) masks CNBC's 'red flags for the economy' and record consumer sentiment lows; if Iran war persists and gas prices remain elevated, labor market weakness likely within 6 months.
market_questions_to_create.trigger_signal: Iran-U.S. escalation, consumer sentiment trend, jobless claims momentum, Fed policy path

market_questions_to_create.question: Will the DRAM ETF (Roundhill Memory) record net outflows in a single month by June 30, 2026?
market_questions_to_create.resolution_source: Roundhill Investments official fund flow data / SEC filings
market_questions_to_create.why_underpriced: $5B AUM in <2 months is classic momentum bubble; memory chip capex cycles are cyclical, and if TSMC or Samsung guidance turns cautious, retail ETF inflows reverse hard. Market consensus is extrapolating April rally indefinitely.
market_questions_to_create.trigger_signal: Memory chip guidance (TSMC, Samsung earnings), AI capex deceleration, PC/data-center refresh cycles

market_questions_to_create.question: Will the EU-U.S. trade agreement be ratified by the July 4, 2026 deadline set by Trump?
market_questions_to_create.resolution_source: Official EU Council / U.S. Trade Representative statements or signed agreement filings
market_questions_to_create.why_underpriced: Trump's July 4 ultimatum is credible (he has tariff authority), but EU legislative processes are slow; market may underestimate probability of last-minute deal or extension, creating optionality for tariff-sensitive stocks (NVDA, chip ETFs).
market_questions_to_create.trigger_signal: EU legislative calendar, Trump tariff tweet escalation, WTO filings, automotive/tech lobby pressure

investment_theses.thesis: Short DXY (U.S. Dollar Index) into Q3 2026 if Iran ceasefire holds and consumer sentiment stabilizes, betting on Fed rate cuts and emerging market carry unwind.
supporting_signals: Record-low U.S. consumer sentiment driven by transient gas price spike (Iran war); if conflict de-escalates, sentiment rebounds and risk appetite returns, weakening USD.
U.S. jobs beat (115K) suggests labor market resilience but CNBC flags 'red flags'; Fed likely to cut rates by September if inflation cools and employment softens.
Stablecoin adoption accelerating (+100% YoY) signals crypto/emerging market traders positioning for EM rallies, which benefit from weaker USD.
investment_theses.counter_view: Iran-U.S. tensions persist or escalate (U.S. struck Iran tankers this week, Rubio expects Iranian response 'today'), pushing safe-haven flows into USD and equities; Trump's EU tariff threat may also support DXY if trade wars worsen.
investment_theses.expression: Short DXY futures or long EUR/USD and emerging currency pairs (e.g., via EEM or FXI); hedge with long-dated UUP puts

investment_theses.thesis: Overweight semiconductor and memory chips (NVDA, MU, LRCX, DRAM ETF) in a tactical 30-day trade, but trim position before August on capex cycle risk.
supporting_signals: DRAM ETF (Roundhill Memory) added $1B in a single day and raised $5B since April 2; retail momentum is on, and institutional AI capex demand (data centers, training runs) will sustain nearterm inflows.

Chinese 36Kr reports on AI infra spending: Tsinghua-backed GPU system architecture startup completed 'several hundred million yuan' in funding; this signals aggressive Chinese capex competition and upstream demand for memory/logic chips.
Ondo (RWA tokenization), Toncoin (TON), and Starknet trending; these imply infrastructure buildout and compute/memory intensity.
investment_theses.counter_view: Memory chip capex is procyclical; if Fed cuts rates too aggressively or recession fears spike, capex guidance will collapse. DRAM ETF's $5B inflow in 8 weeks is textbook retail bubble; redemptions could cascade by mid-Q3.
investment_theses.expression: Long NVDA, MU, or LRCX calls (30-delta, 60 DTE); or accumulate on dips below 20-day MA; trim 30% on any single-day 5%+ rally. Avoid DRAM ETF directly due to tail risk.

investment_theses.thesis: Long Bitcoin (via spot or 3-month perps) if Iran-U.S. ceasefire holds by May 10, 2026, targeting $85K–$90K on safe-haven reversal and stablecoin demand recovery.
supporting_signals: BTC retreated below $80K on $300M liquidations and macro fear, but stablecoin card spend (+100% YoY) and Coinbase legislation momentum suggest institutional on-ramps are live.
U.S. jobs beat (115K vs 55K consensus) contradicts recession narrative; if labor holds and consumer sentiment recovers post-Iran deal, risk-on flows resume and BTC rallies.
Crypto infrastructure (Anthropic Mythos, Canvas breach) is triggering institutional urgency around non-custodial settlement; Bitcoin adoption as collateral/hedge accelerates in this environment.
investment_theses.counter_view: Iran escalation continues (U.S. already struck Iranian tankers; Rubio expects response 'today'); gas prices stay elevated, consumer sentiment worsens, and recession fears drive risk-off. BTC could test $70K or lower if U.S. cuts rates but also pushes fiscal austerity.
investment_theses.expression: BTC spot (buy dips to $77K–$78K) or 3-month BTC perps (5–10x leverage on a 10% allocation); hedge with long-term gold or TLT calls if geopolitical risk widens.

investment_theses.thesis: Long ETH relative to BTC (via ETH/BTC ratio long or synthetic long ETH + short BTC) into Q3 2026, betting on Ethereum staking yields, L2 adoption (Starknet, Ondo), and AI on-chain activity outpacing Bitcoin narrative.
supporting_signals: Starknet (STRK) and Ondo (RWA) are trending; both live on Ethereum ecosystem and signal institutional capital moving to Ethereum for real-world asset tokenization and scalability.
Anthropic Mythos and Canvas breach fears are driving institutions to on-chain settlement and non-custodial crypto; Ethereum's smart contract richness outperforms Bitcoin's store-of-value thesis.
Chinese 36Kr reports accelerating AI infrastructure funding; Ethereum is the leading EVM for AI agent contracts and DeFi; this creates upside versus Bitcoin's passive reserve status.
investment_theses.counter_view: Bitcoin narrative strengthens if geopolitical turmoil persists (Iran war, trade wars); BTC outperforms as pure hedge. Ethereum's scaling has not yet translated to material on-chain volume or fee growth; Solana and TON may cannibalize L2 adoption.
investment_theses.expression: Long ETH/BTC ratio (via perpetual or calendar spreads); or long ETH spot + short BTC spot (synthetic pair trade). Minimize directional BTC risk by maintaining 1:1 hedge ratio.

investment_theses.thesis: Short consumer discretionary (XLY) and long consumer staples (XLP) into June 2026, reflecting deteriorating consumer sentiment (record low in May) and potential for delayed recession as geopolitical shocks ripple through energy and supply chains.
supporting_signals: CNBC reports consumer sentiment fell to 'fresh record low' in May due to surging gas prices from Iran war; this is a real, measurable demand destruction signal for discretionary spending.
U.S. jobs beat today masks CNBC's 'red flags for the economy'; if layoffs accelerate in June-July (classic recession lag), discretionary weakness persists even as wage growth slows.

Russia-Ukraine ceasefire breach (BBC report) and Iran-U.S. escalation (U.S. struck tankers) suggest geopolitical volatility will persist; energy prices likely to remain elevated, further pressuring consumer.
investment_theses.counter_view: Jobs beat suggests labor market resilience; consumers have high savings rates and credit quality. If Iran ceasefire holds and gas prices fall, sentiment recovers quickly and discretionary rebounds. XLY may outperform if Fed cuts rates (lower borrowing costs).
investment_theses.expression: Short XLY calls or long XLY puts (30-delta, 60 DTE); long XLP calls or spot accumulation; or trade the XLY/XLP ratio short via call spreads.

FLAG_CHINESE_SOURCES.note: 36Kr surfaces THREE stories ahead of English media attention:

story_1.title: eVTOL/eCTOL capex surge: Weixinhaang (维新宇航) completes tens of millions seed+ funding for 7-ton eVTOL first flight in July 2026
story_1.english_media_lag: Zero English news coverage; 36Kr exclusively reported. Signals: Chinese low-altitude mobility capex is accelerating (memory, compute, avionics demand upstream).
story_1.relevance: Confirms chip/memory capex thesis for DRAM/NVDA; Chinese AI infrastructure buildout includes autonomous flight systems.

story_2.title: Plaud AI recorder valuation jumped to $2B (from $1B mid-2025) with Tencent backing; hardware collaboration with Tencent Meeting
story_2.english_media_lag: Zero English coverage; 36Kr exclusive. Signals: AI hardware (recorder + meeting integration) is a $2B+ category in China; Tencent is diversifying beyond software into devices.
story_2.relevance: Bullish for Ondo (RWA/AI infrastructure), bearish for pure software multiples; suggests hardware-software convergence in China AI stack.

story_3.title: Solid-state battery (纯锂新能源) completes Pre-A+ funding for mass production; Aizhuang State-owned Capital backing signals policy push for EV battery innovation
story_3.english_media_lag: Zero English coverage; 36Kr exclusive. Signals: China's EV battery supply chain is consolidating; state capital is backing solid-state commercialization ahead of 2027-28 launch cycles.
story_3.relevance: Directionally bullish for EV sentiment and memory capex (BMS, thermal management compute); could support DRAM/memory thesis if battery management systems add AI acceleration.
